arts.ttl: Birmingham City tipped to destroy Wolves and on track for EFL record
arts.sum: The prediction from CasinoHawks' latest League One Supercomputer has Blues finishing on 108 points, which would see them topple Wolves and Reading’s long-standing records. Birmingham City have been tipped to shatter the League One points record Wolverhampton Wanderers set in 2014.

arts.sum: Birmingham City have regularly enjoyed sell-out attendances at St Andrew's @ Knighthead Park this season Blues have been backed by sell-out crowds on a fortnightly basis this season with long suffering fans flocking to the revamped St Andrew’s @ Knighthead Park.

arts.ttl: Former Birmingham City defender 'extremely active' as he waits for new manager job
arts.sum: Former Birmingham City defender Jerry Gill has declared himself ready to return to the dugout, three months after leaving Bath City - and he considers himself a 'more rounded and improved' coach after spending his time out of the game wisely. Former Birmingham City defender Jerry Gill spent seven years at Bath City but is ready for another challenge in management

arts.ttl: Birmingham City need Alfie May to do what they signed him for now
arts.sum: May isn’t expected to be Stansfield, but Blues do need the version of League One’s reigning Golden Boot winner that we saw in August – or very briefly in January when he netted three goals in two games against Stockport and Wigan. Ten games have passed for May since he scored a brace at Wigan Athletic on January 4, making this the longest run without a goal of his Blues career.

arts.ttl: Alfie May’s time is now after £1,200 refusal costs Birmingham City at Reading
arts.sum: Yes, Dykes should have scored, and Blues had a couple of other chances that could have been taken on another day, but Reading are among the best teams we have seen in League One this season. A draw was a fair result, albeit Lyndon Dykes could have turned one point into three for Blues before he went off injured.
